properties.fullDescription: The Hinton is a four-bedroom detached home designed around everyday family life. It's a practical, well-considered layout. On the ground floor, a separate living room connects through double doors to the open-plan kitchen and dining area. The High specification kitchen includes integrated appliances and an eye level oven as standard, with a utility room off the kitchen and bi-fold doors from the dining area opening directly onto the rear garden. There's also a cloakroom and under-stairs storage in the hallway. Upstairs, three of the four bedrooms have fitted wardrobes, The principal bedroom, Bedroom 2 and Bedroom 4, with fitted cupboard space in Bedroom 3. The principal bedroom has an en suite with shower. All four bedrooms share a family bathroom. The Hinton comes with a garage, dedicated parking and a 10-year new homes warranty. The home is also heated by an air source heat pump, a low-carbon alternative to a gas boiler that's cheaper to run. properties.fullDescription: The Brook is a three-bedroom home that keeps things straightforward. A separate living room at the front, an open-plan kitchen and dining room with French doors to the garden at the rear, and three bedrooms arranged simply upstairs. It's a layout that suits buyers who want space that's easy to live in without compromise: no awkward through-rooms, no wasted hallways, just a home that works from the moment you move in. Upstairs, the principal bedroom has a fitted wardrobe and en suite shower room. Bedroom 2 is a comfortable double. Bedroom 3 works well as a single bedroom or home office. All three bedrooms share a family bathroom. The Brook comes with parking for two cars, an air source heat pump, underfloor heating to the ground floor and a 10-year new homes warranty. properties.fullDescription: The Chelmer is one of the larger homes at Chediston Place with 1,454 sq ft of thoughtfully arranged space, with a garage. Downstairs, the living room opens directly onto the garden through bi-fold doors, while the kitchen and dining area, connected by double doors, has its own set of French doors to the outside. Two sets of doors to the garden means the ground floor flows in a way that genuinely blurs inside and outside. Integrated appliances and a separate utility room complete the kitchen. There's also a study that works as a proper workspace, not just a corner of a bedroom. Upstairs, the principal bedroom has a double wardrobe and its own en suite shower room. The remaining three bedrooms share a well-proportioned family bathroom. Two of those bedrooms include fitted wardrobes. This home is heated by an air source heat pump, a low-carbon alternative to a gas boiler that costs less to run and needs no fuel deliveries. It comes with an EPC B rating and a 10-year new homes warranty. Chediston Place sits on the edge of Halesworth, a market town with a working high street, independent shops and a theatre — with the Suffolk coast less than 10 miles away. properties.fullDescription: Where the Dorley stands out among the four-bedroom homes at Chediston Place is its living room, an 18ft5 x 10ft6 room with a bay window that gives it a scale and character you don't often find in a new build. It's a home that suits buyers who want their main reception room to do real work: space for a large sofa and enough room that it doesn't feel compromised by furniture. The rest of the ground floor is equally well considered. To the rear, an open-plan kitchen and dining room benefits from bi-fold doors to the rear garden. A utility room sits between the kitchen and the garage, giving you a practical buffer for muddy boots, coats and laundry before you reach the living space and direct internal access to the garage. There's also a cloakroom and storage cupboard in the hallway. Upstairs, all four bedrooms have fitted wardrobes including the principal bedroom, which has its own en suite shower room. Bedrooms 2, 3 and 4 share a family bathroom. The Dorley comes with a garage, dedicated parking and a 10-year new homes warranty. EPC rating predicted B. Completion Spring 2026. properties.title: Residential development for sale
properties.fullDescription: Ground floor retail premises - comprising a former hairdressing salon with kitchen and WCs.Residential accommodation arranged over the first and second floor and comprising an entrance lobby, kitchen, bathroom, sitting room and two bedrooms. Established garden and courtyard to the rear. No forward chain. LocationThe property is set along The Thoroughfare, overlooking St Mary’s Church, in the centre of this popular market town. Halesworth has a variety of shops catering for virtually every day-to-day need. There is a primary school, library, doctors’ surgery, Co-operative supermarket and numerous cafes, pubs and restaurants. The town also benefits from The Cut, a centre for arts in the community which offers music, theatre, dance, comedy, cinema, workshops and art exhibitions to the local area. Halesworth is also well served with transport connections, having a railway station with services via Ipswich to London’s Liverpool Street station. To the east of Halesworth lies the Heritage Coast which is renowned for its wide variety of leisure opportunities including the well regarded seaside resort, Southwold, (9 miles), historic Dunwich and the RSPB Reserve at Minsmere. DirectionsProceeding into Halesworth from the south on the A144 London Road, bear left at the roundabout onto The Thoroughfare. Continue along The Thoroughfare where the property will be found straight ahead almost opposite the church. For those using the What3Words app: ///proven.finds.twit DescriptionThis Grade II Listed mixed use property, located in the centre of Halesworth and overlooking St Mary’s Church, offers an exciting renovation and refurbishment project. According to the Listing Schedule the property is believed to date from the late 16th Century and is of primarily timber frame construction set beneath plaintile roofs. We understand the established use is as a ground floor commercial premises, which previously operated as a hairdressing salon. The ground floor accommodation extends to approximately 470 sq ft (43.8 sqm) and comprises a partly subdivided salon/retailing area on the ground floor, together with a kitchen and WCs at the rear. An external staircase provides access to the residential accommodation above. This comprises an entrance lobby, kitchen, bathroom, spacious sitting room with vaulted ceiling, a double bedroom and a single bedroom on the second floor. OutsideThere is an enclosed courtyard area to the rear of the property beyond which is an established area of garden that contains a number of mature shrubs and which is partly enclosed by high level red brick wall. Prospective purchasers should note that no pedestrian or vehicular right of way will be offered across the neighbouring property, 39 Thoroughfare, and the purchasers will be obliged to enclose the garden with an appropriate wall or fence within 3 months of the sale completing. properties.fullDescription: Beautifully established private rear garden arranged over tiers, with sunny patio seating areas Attractive communal gardens with seating areas, mature planting, and a pergola overflowing with wisteria Peaceful tucked-away setting just off Halesworth marketplace, and an allocated parking space directly beside the property Attik are delighted to offer this well-presented two-bedroom home for the over 55s, tucked away in a peaceful yet highly convenient position just off Halesworth’s marketplace. Offering well-balanced accommodation, a private and established rear garden, allocated parking, and attractive communal grounds, all within easy reach of the town centre, railway station and the Suffolk coast. Contact the team to view today.Tucked Away Living In The Heart Of Halesworth...6 Old Brewery Yard enjoys a wonderfully convenient yet peaceful setting, approached via a track leading directly from the town’s historic marketplace.Forming part of a well-established over-55s development, the property sits within attractive communal grounds, where mature planting and a striking wisteria create an inviting first impression. There is an allocated parking space along with a shared seating area, offering a pleasant sense of community without compromising privacy.The property itself provides well-balanced accommodation, beginning with a welcoming entrance hall. The kitchen is fitted with integrated cooking appliances and enjoys a pleasant outlook over the communal gardens. The main sitting room is a comfortable space, centred around a fireplace, and opens into a bright conservatory which works particularly well as a dining area, with views and direct access to the garden.There are two bedrooms, both of good proportions, with the principal bedroom benefiting from built-in storage and views to the rear. A well-appointed shower room completes the internal layout.To the outside, the rear garden is a real highlight. Arranged over tiers, it combines patio seating areas with established planting, creating a private and sheltered space that is not overlooked. There is also useful side access and a shed for storage. There are also well-tended communal grounds with seating areas, one covered by a pergola with a beautiful Wisteria.Old Brewery Yard is ideally positioned within easy walking distance of Halesworth’s excellent range of independent shops, cafés and everyday amenities. The railway station provides connections to Ipswich and onward to London Liverpool Street, while the Suffolk Heritage Coast, including Southwold and Walberswick, is within easy reach.Overall, a well-located and thoughtfully arranged home, offering comfortable living in a highly convenient position close to both town and coast.Agents Notes...A pre-recorded walkaround tour is available for this property.Over 55s only. properties.fullDescription: Characterful end-of-terrace cottage within Halesworth's desirable conservation area. Spacious sitting/dining room with exposed oak beams, floor boards, and brickwork, and a wood-burning stove. Two first-floor bedrooms, including a generous principal bedroom and flexible second room. Easy walk to independent shops, cafés, pubs, restaurants, supermarkets and the recreation park. Just a short drive from Southwold and the Suffolk Heritage Coast, with rail links from Halesworth to Ipswich and London. Low-maintenance cottage lifestyle, leaving more time to enjoy Halesworth's thriving community and the nearby Suffolk Heritage Coast. Attik are delighted to present this charming two-bedroom period-cottage located just moments from Halesworth town centre. This beautiful home features exposed brickwork, timber beams, a cosy wood-burning stove, and original floorboards. Inside, you’ll find a welcoming living space, a functional kitchen, a bathroom with full suite, and two delightful bedrooms with exposed beams. The property also benefits from a conservatory, a private shed, and residents’ permit parking. All this, just a short walk from shops, pubs, the church, and the vibrant marketplace. To view please call the team today!The Characterful End Terraced Cottage...Behind its traditional façade and tucked within Halesworth's attractive conservation area, this characterful end-of-terrace cottage offers an opportunity to enjoy market town living with the Suffolk Heritage Coast just a short drive away. Full of period charm and offered with no onward chain, it would make an ideal first home, weekend retreat or investment.Step inside and the welcoming sitting/dining room immediately sets the tone. Exposed oak beams, brickwork and Suffolk floor bricks celebrate the cottage's heritage, while a brick fireplace housing a wood-burning stove creates a cosy focal point for the colder months. Traditional timber doors and character features throughout add warmth and authenticity that are becoming increasingly difficult to find.The kitchen is practical and functional as it stands, offering plenty of potential for a future owner to update and personalise over time without detracting from the cottage's charm. Beyond, there's a lobby to the bathroom, with a white 3-piece suite, and to the conservatory, which, although in need of some tlc, it provides flexible additional space, equally suited as a garden room, dining area, hobby room or peaceful place to unwind. Alternatively, subject to individual requirements, a future owner may choose to replace it with a more generous courtyard garden.Upstairs, the principal bedroom is generously proportioned, while the second bedroom offers excellent versatility as a guest room, dressing room or home office-perfect for modern living.Outside, there is a brick-built shed to the rear, and residents' permit parking is available on the street, with visitor permits obtainable through East Suffolk Council.Positioned just a short stroll from the he

The location offers the best of town living, with the independent shops, cafés and amenities of the Thoroughfare within around five minutes on foot, while Halesworth railway station – providing links to Ipswich and onward connections to London – is also close by. The beautiful Suffolk Heritage Coast, including Southwold and Walberswick, is only a short drive away.The property itself presents an attractive period frontage and immediately reveals its historic origins once inside, with exposed beams and a wealth of original features throughout. The sitting room is a welcoming space, centred around a wood-burning stove and enjoying views to the front, creating a warm and comfortable setting.To the rear, the kitchen offers space for dining, with room for a table and everyday appliances, while a window overlooks the courtyard garden. The home presents generous single‐level proportions, a bright semi open plan layout, modern refitted kitchen and bathrooms, and two well placed bedrooms overlooking the garden. Fully owned solar panels, excellent storage and an integral garage add everyday practicality, while the landscaped rear garden with its raised terrace brings a welcoming outdoor element. Families benefit from nearby schooling, with Edgar Sewter Primary School and other options in nearby towns. The enclosed porch opens into a wide hallway, where two substantial storage cupboards offer everyday practicality and help maintain a calm, uncluttered feel.The principal reception space sits to the front and is arranged in a semi‐open plan layout that encourages a natural flow between areas. properties.fullDescription: Semi-detached bungalow positioned on a generous-size plot, down a residential road in the Suffolk town of Halesworth Opportunity to modernise the interior and extend at the rear, creating a home that can adapt to your own lifestyle preferences (stpp) Spacious living room accentuated by a traditional brick-built fireplace and sliding doors that open out to the patio, inviting relaxation and entertaining Light-filled dining room that opens into the kitchen, fitted with units, an integrated oven and under-counter areas for appliances Two double bedrooms offering comfort and privacy, along with a bathroom comprising of a classic three-piece suite A large garden featuring a paved patio for seating, a sweeping lawn, a timber storage shed, established shrubbery and tall trees for privacy A maintained front garden, a paved driveway providing off-road parking and a garage for storage use Easy access to the towns amenities including shops, restaurants, schools and transport links Guide price £260,000-£280,000There is an immediate sense of space and ease here, with open skies and the gentle backdrop of surrounding fields shaping a setting that feels both open and unhurried. Offered chain free, this semi-detached bungalow in the Suffolk town of Halesworth occupies a generous plot along a residential road, combining comfortable, well-proportioned accommodation with the opportunity to update and extend (stpp). With light-filled living spaces, two double bedrooms and a particularly generous garden suited to both everyday living and entertaining, the property offers a home that can readily adapt to changing needs and personal style.HalesworthDukes Drive is located within a quiet, predominantly residential part of Halesworth, a small Suffolk market town known for its relaxed pace and strong sense of community. Halesworth itself centres around the pedestrianised Thoroughfare, which provides a selection of independent shops, cafés and everyday services, along with a regular market and a well-used arts venue at The Cut. For food shopping, there is a Co-op supermarket in the town, as well as a larger Tesco store on the outskirts, covering most day-to-day needs.Families are served by a number of nearby schools, including Edgar Sewter Community Primary School and Holton St Peter Community Primary School, both within a short distance, while secondary education is typically provided by Sir John Leman High School in nearby Beccles or Bungay High School.In terms of transport, Halesworth railway station lies roughly a mile from Dukes Drive and offers services via Ipswich with onward connections to London Liverpool Street, while local bus routes link the town to surrounding villages and coastal areas.
